meta.title: Cashflow Academy
meta.description: Structured course content for the GYC Cashflow Academy

sections.title: Strategy
sections.description: Build your investment thesis, understand asset classes, and develop a clear plan before deploying capital.

sections.title: Deal Flow
sections.description: Learn how to source, evaluate, and filter deals so you only spend time on opportunities worth pursuing.

sections.title: Execution
sections.description: Close deals with confidence — due diligence, underwriting, legal structures, and post-close asset management.

lessons.description: An overview of how the academy is structured, what to expect, and how to get the most out of every module.
A short orientation to the course structure, learning path, and what to focus on first as you move through the academy.

lessons.lesson_title: Understanding the Players in Passive Investing
lessons.description: A simple map of the key roles in passive investing, from LPs and sponsors to operators, lenders, and other stakeholders.

lessons.lesson_title: Embracing Your Role As A Money Manager
lessons.description: A mindset lesson on taking ownership of capital allocation decisions and approaching investing like a thoughtful money manager.
How to articulate what you're looking for, what asset classes fit your goals, and why clarity upfront saves months of wasted effort.
An overview of how to sharpen your investment criteria so you can evaluate opportunities with more clarity and consistency.
How to pressure-test and refine your thesis as you gain experience, see more deals, and clarify your long-term goals.
Break down IRR, equity multiples, cash-on-cash, and learn which metrics actually matter for passive investors.
A primer on alternative investments, what qualifies as an alternative, and why these assets can play a role in a diversified portfolio.
Why multifamily remains the backbone of most LP portfolios — occupancy drivers, rent growth, and value-add mechanics.
A practical comparison of funds and syndications, including how each structure works and when one may fit better than the other.
An explanation of how risk changes across the capital stack and what that means for return expectations and investor positioning.
A breakdown of five common ways operators create value in real estate investments and how each strategy affects risk and upside.
Emerging asset classes that offer diversification — what to look for and the unique risks of each.
How lending-side investments work, preferred return structures, and when debt beats equity.
Allocation frameworks, vintage year diversification, and how to think about portfolio construction as a passive investor.
A portfolio-planning overview focused on defining your targets so your allocation decisions line up with your goals.
A practical walkthrough of how to build an allocation plan across investment buckets, time horizons, and risk profiles.

lessons.lesson_title: Tax Strategy for Real Estate Investors
lessons.description: K-1s, depreciation, cost segregation, and 1031 exchanges explained in plain English for passive investors.
A quick breakdown of how alternative investments can support diversification, cash flow, inflation protection, and long-term portfolio resilience.

lessons.lesson_title: The Money Pie | The Only 3 Outcomes When Investing
lessons.description: A simple framework for understanding the three core outcomes every investment can produce and how that shapes allocation and portfolio decisions.
A high-level review of major asset classes, how they differ, and where each can fit within a thoughtful investment strategy.

lessons.lesson_title: Where to Find Quality Deal Flow
lessons.description: The best channels for sourcing syndications, funds, and direct deals — and how to filter signal from noise.
An overview of how to spot promising opportunities, narrow the universe, and focus your attention on deals worth deeper review.
How to build a repeatable deal pipeline so opportunities keep flowing without relying on random outreach or one-off luck.
A practical look at how to organize inbound opportunities, maintain momentum, and keep your pipeline moving without overwhelm.

lessons.lesson_title: Why Deal Flow Is The Foundation For Investment Success
lessons.description: Why consistent access to quality deal flow shapes long-term investing results and helps you make stronger decisions over time.

lessons.lesson_title: Deal Flow Diplomacy | How to Decline Offers Without Closing Doors
lessons.description: How to turn down deals gracefully while preserving relationships and keeping the door open for better-fit opportunities later.
Track record analysis, reference checks, and red flags to watch for when vetting operators and general partners.
An introduction to assessing operators, including what to look for early and how to separate strong sponsors from weak ones.
How to quickly parse a pitch deck, identify the key assumptions, and spot what's missing before diving deeper.
A repeatable framework to quickly decide if a deal is worth your time — before spending hours on diligence.
A high-level framework for evaluating deals so you can move from initial interest to focused, disciplined screening.
A guide to the warning signs that often show up early in weak opportunities, questionable assumptions, or poorly aligned deals.
The core components a strong investment deck should include so you can quickly judge clarity, completeness, and professionalism.
A comparison of strong and weak deck presentation so you can spot quality signals and identify what may be missing or misleading.
How to evaluate submarket fundamentals — job growth, population trends, supply pipeline, and rent comps.
A practical overview of the main risk categories passive investors face and how to think about them before committing capital.
Which financial metrics matter most when screening deals and how to avoid being distracted by numbers that look impressive but say little.
Common pitfalls that catch passive investors off guard and what to watch for before you move from interest to commitment.
Pref equity, common equity, waterfall splits, and co-invest terms — what each structure means for your returns.
Walk through a real multifamily deal submission — analyzing the deck, financials, and sponsor track record in real time.
Analyzing a BTR fund — the economics of new construction, lease-up risk, and what makes this structure different.
A step-by-step checklist for evaluating any passive real estate investment — from financials to legal docs.
What to look for in the private placement memorandum and operating agreement — the clauses that actually matter.
The 7 assumptions sponsors get wrong most often — and how to stress-test projections yourself.
A framework for thinking about diligence as a spectrum, from quick screening to deeper validation as conviction increases.
An overview of how to use outside input, experience, and trusted feedback loops to validate assumptions before moving forward.
How to run stronger reference checks, ask better questions, and use qualitative feedback to de-risk investment decisions.

lessons.lesson_title: Understanding Fees In Real Estate Investments
lessons.description: A breakdown of common fees in real estate investments and how fee structures can affect alignment, net returns, and risk.
The logistics of investing — accreditation verification, subscription agreements, and how the capital call process works.
A high-level walkthrough of turning your investment plan into action once diligence is complete and decisions are ready to be implemented.

lessons.lesson_title: How To Use The LP Investment Schedule
lessons.description: How to use an LP investment schedule to stay organized across opportunities, commitments, timing, and follow-through.
LLCs, trusts, SDIRAs, and solo 401(k)s — which entity to use and when it matters for asset protection and taxes.
What to look for in quarterly reports, distribution notices, and how to know when a deal is off-track.
Capital calls, loan maturities, and restructures — what to do when an investment doesn't go as planned.
Recorded member Q&A covering sponsor vetting, portfolio allocation, and real-time deal analysis.
